intro: Rossioglossum powellii, also known as Powell's Ross's orchid, is a species of orchid native to Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rossioglossum powellii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
